creator: sweetpea-freebsd@tentacle.net
text:
When displaying web pages with particular background images, the browser window ends up being overlaid with other parts of the screen.  It happens with or without extensions installed, with a freshly created profile.

An example of a very simple page with repeating background is here:

http://img171.imageshack.us/img171/2972/brokenbrowseryb1.png

This problem appeared in Firefox 3; it was not present in Firefox 2.

I am using the radeon driver, board name M10 NT [FireGL Mobility T2].

Fix: 

No fix, but a workaround is to install the Web Developer toolbar, and select Images->Hide Background Images.
How-To-Repeat: It's possible to create a very simple HTML document, containing a background image.  If the image is small enough to tile, then the problem will show up -- but, it only happens with certain images.  I have found an image that causes this problem; it can be found here:

http://img401.imageshack.us/img401/3184/xxak3.jpg

I originally thought it happened with any 8-bit JPEG, but when I created one, I couldn't reproduce the problem.
